title: Pediocactus horripilus

The scientific classification of Pediocactus horripilus is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Asterids, order Caryophyllales, family Cactaceae, subfamily Pachycereeae, genus Pediocactus, and species P. horripilus. intro: Pediocactus horripilus, also known as the spiny Pediocactus, is a species of cactus native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its small size, rounded body, and numerous spines. Traditionally, Pediocactus horripilus has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues, due to its potential antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Pediocactus horripilus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Pediocactus horripilus is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in topical applications to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
